commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh My table, if the king shall please so to order. (Sanchez) It would seem that Siba did not properly understand the king. (Calmet) The Septuagint, Syriac, and Arabic, read, perhaps more correctly u, "his "instead of i, "my table "in shulchani. "And Memphibosthai ate at David's table, as one "(Haydock) He did not merely eat of the king's table, (ver. 13,) but at it, super mensam, as the Hebrew, Chaldean, and Septuagint express. (Menochius).
